channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple Commits $500 Billion to US Manufacturing and R&D
item.description: Tech giant Apple announces largest spend commitment to date, investing $500 billion in American manufacturing, engineering, and AI efforts over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Grace Snelling, Fast Company

item.title: China 'sinks' 400 servers equivalent to 30,000 gaming PCs as it powers ahead with massive underwater data center project - but I wonder what GPU they use
item.description: China’s underwater AI push sees 400 servers deployed beneath the sea, with plans for 40,000 more in years ahead.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Elizabeth Holmes fails to overturn her Theranos fraud conviction
item.description: The three-judge panel in San Francisco also upheld the fraud conviction of Holmes’ former business partner and lover Ramesh “Sunny” Balwani.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Warren questions Commerce secretary on Musk access to data on competitors
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) on Monday questioned Secretary of Commerce Howard Lutnick over the access tech billionaire Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) has to his competitors and business partners.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How ‘lore’ became the internet’s favorite way to overshare
item.description: Lore isn’t just for games like The Elder Scrolls or films like The Lord of the Rings—online, it has evolved into something entirely new. The Old English word made the shortlist for Oxford University Presss’ 2024 Word of the Year (though it ultimately lost to brain rot).
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Comedians are turning to LinkedIn to find a new audience
item.description: Ben Sweeny, the salesman-turned-comedian behind that online persona Corporate Sween, says that bosses should waterboard their employees. “Some companies drown their employees with boring surveys and useless questionnaires,” he proclaims in a satirical video posted to LinkedIn a few months ago.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Apple’s latest iOS update improves CarPlay, but not everyone will be able to access it
item.description: A third row of apps has appeared on some user's infotainment systems following the recent iOS 18.4 beta update.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: I can't say I'm surprised, but Nvidia's RTX 5070 leaked benchmark reveals up to 20% performance boost over RTX 4070
item.description: It may not live up to Nvidia's RTX 4090 performance claim, but new benchmark leaks suggest a reasonable performance uplift for the RTX 5070 over its predecessor.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: A GPU or a CPU with 4TB HBM-class memory? Nope, you're not dreaming, Sandisk is working on such a monstrous product
item.description: Sandisk has unveiled High Bandwidth Flash (HBF) for AI workloads which will have a major impact on the $100 billion HBM market.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: xAI could sign a $5 billion deal with Dell for thousands of servers with Nvidia's GB200 Blackwell AI GPU accelerators
item.description: Reports claim Dell is about to secure a $5 billion deal to supply Elon Musk's xAI with yet more AI servers.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Race to 100TB HDD heats up as Seagate pulls rug under Western Digital, Toshiba feet by acquiring HAMR-specialist
item.description: Seagate is set to buy a specialist in HAMR HDD production, a move which will be bad news for rivals Western Digital and Toshiba.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: The government or 4chan? The White House’s social media account is sparking outrage
item.description: The official White House social media account is under fire for posts that resemble something typically found on the internet forum 4chan. A post shared on February 14, styled like a Valentine’s card, read, “Roses are red, violets are blue, come here illegally and we’ll deport you.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How Wikipedia became a political lightning rod
item.description: Wikipedia has faced political threats for years, but this time, it may be at a breaking point. Republicans have ramped up attacks against Wikipedia as yet another “woke” institution.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Photos: The 2025 Silicon Valley Auto Show
item.description: Visitors of all ages explored the latest vehicles from 17 different manufacturers, including Toyota, Kia, Porsche, Lexus and Infiniti.
item.dc:creator: Ray Chavez, The Mercury News

item.title: Amazon concedes that Chime SDK makes far more sense than the Chime application itself
item.description: Amazon’s announcement on Wednesday that it is abandoning its Chime collaboration app, while stressing that it will double down on the far more successful Chime software development kit (SDK), was an example of Amazon being Amazon. It knows what it does well, and where to focus.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How artificial intelligence is energizing information technology
item.description: "Data is getting even more gravity in this space because the more data you have, the more accuracy these parameters get in your model," said Bill Vass. The post How artificial intelligence is energizing information technology first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported Friday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Majority of Americans dislike Musk, Zuckerberg: Poll
item.description: A Pew Research Center poll shows 54% of Americans have an "unfavorable" view of Musk, while 67% have an "unfavorable" view of Zuckerberg.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Apple terminates UK data protection after government overreach
item.description: As of 10 a.m. (ET) today, Apple is no longer offering new users in the UK the chance to enable Advanced Data Protection. Essentially, anyone who now wants to enable the feature to protect their data against criminal or state surveillance or exfiltration will no longer be able to do so.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
